ruleset.name: Pathfinder 2nd Edition
ruleset.license: Original mechanical references; Pathfinder 2e ruleset content under Paizo's ORC license. Spell, class, and monster names belong to Paizo Publishing.
ruleset.summary: Pathfinder 2nd Edition is a tightly mathematical d20 fantasy system. A check is 1d20 + ability modifier + proficiency bonus + item bonus vs. a Difficulty Class, with four degrees of success: critical success at +10 over the DC, success at meeting it, failure under it, critical failure at -10 below. The three-action economy and Multiple Attack Penalty give every turn meaningful tactical choice.

Trivial.description: Untrained character can manage with average stats.

Low.description: Trained at character's level expects to succeed.

Moderate.description: The standard challenge for an at-level expert.

High.description: Pushes a master character; a trained one will need luck.

Severe.description: A serious test even for a Master at level.

attributes.name: Strength
attributes.description: Raw physical force. Adds to Athletics, melee attack rolls with non-finesse weapons, and melee damage.

attributes.name: Dexterity
attributes.description: Agility, balance, fine motor control. Adds to AC (capped by armor's Dex cap), Reflex saves, ranged attacks, and finesse melee attacks.

attributes.name: Constitution
attributes.description: Health and endurance. Adds to Hit Points each level and to Fortitude saves.

attributes.name: Intelligence
attributes.description: Memory, reasoning, education. Grants extra trained skills and Lores at character creation; adds to skills like Arcana, Crafting, Society.

attributes.name: Wisdom
attributes.description: Awareness, intuition, common sense. Adds to Perception, Will saves, and skills like Medicine, Nature, Religion, Survival.

attributes.name: Charisma
attributes.description: Presence, persuasion, force of personality. Adds to social skills (Diplomacy, Deception, Intimidation, Performance, Society) and to spell DCs for Charisma-based casters.

attributes.name: Level
attributes.description: Character level (1-20). Drives the proficiency math for trained-and-above skills: Trained = level + 2, Expert = level + 4, Master = level + 6, Legendary = level + 8.

skills.name: Acrobatics
skills.description: Tumbling, balancing, escaping grabs. Trained-only actions: Maneuver in Flight.

skills.name: Arcana
skills.description: Knowledge of arcane magic and creatures. Trained-only: Identify Magic, Learn a Spell, Decipher Writing of arcane origin.

skills.name: Athletics
skills.description: Climbing, jumping, swimming, forceful maneuvers (Grapple, Trip, Shove, Disarm). High Jump and Long Jump are signature actions.

skills.name: Crafting
skills.description: Making and repairing items. Trained-only: Craft, Repair, Identify Alchemy.

skills.name: Deception
skills.description: Lying, disguise, feinting in combat. Trained-only: Impersonate.

skills.name: Diplomacy
skills.description: Making requests, gathering information, swaying attitudes. Always available untrained.

skills.name: Intimidation
skills.description: Coercing or demoralizing. Demoralize is a frequent combat action.

skills.name: Lore
skills.description: A specific narrow expertise the player chose at character creation (Sailing Lore, Goblin Lore, Absalom Lore, Carpentry Lore, etc.). Each Lore is a separate trained skill; the GM allows it for narrow Recall Knowledge questions.

skills.name: Medicine
skills.description: First aid, treating poison/disease. Trained-only: Treat Disease, Treat Poison, Treat Wounds.

skills.name: Nature
skills.description: Knowledge of plants, animals, weather. Trained-only: Command an Animal, Identify Magic of primal origin.

skills.name: Occultism
skills.description: Knowledge of the occult — esoteric philosophy, ghosts, aberrations. Trained-only: Identify Magic of occult origin.

skills.name: Performance
skills.description: Music, oratory, dance, acting. Trained-only: Perform (extended).

skills.name: Religion
skills.description: Knowledge of deities, dogmas, the planes. Trained-only: Identify Magic of divine origin.

skills.name: Society
skills.description: History, etiquette, languages, current affairs. Trained-only: Decipher Writing, Subsist (in civilization).

skills.name: Stealth
skills.description: Hiding and sneaking. Used to oppose Perception when avoiding detection.

skills.name: Survival
skills.description: Wilderness skills: tracking, foraging, weathering. Trained-only: Cover Tracks, Track.

skills.name: Thievery
skills.description: Picking locks, picking pockets, disabling traps. Trained-only: Disable Device, Pick a Lock.

derivedStats.name: Hit Points
derivedStats.formula: (class HP per level + CON_mod) per character level + ancestry HP at level 1

derivedStats.name: Armor Class
derivedStats.formula: 10 + DEX_mod (capped by armor) + armor proficiency bonus + armor item bonus

derivedStats.name: Class DC
derivedStats.formula: 10 + class proficiency bonus + key ability modifier

derivedStats.name: Spell DC
derivedStats.formula: 10 + spell proficiency bonus + casting ability modifier

derivedStats.name: Perception
derivedStats.formula: WIS_mod + perception proficiency bonus + item bonus

derivedStats.name: Fortitude Save
derivedStats.formula: CON_mod + fortitude proficiency bonus + item bonus

derivedStats.name: Reflex Save
derivedStats.formula: DEX_mod + reflex proficiency bonus + item bonus

derivedStats.name: Will Save
derivedStats.formula: WIS_mod + will proficiency bonus + item bonus

derivedStats.name: Hero Points
derivedStats.formula: Start each session at 1; gain more for heroic moments. Cap 3.

values.label: Off-Guard
values.description: -2 circumstance penalty to AC. Used to be 'flat-footed'. Triggered by being flanked, prone, hidden from, or many maneuvers.

values.label: Frightened
values.description: Status penalty equal to the value to all checks and DCs. Decreases by 1 at the end of each turn (or by other means).

values.label: Sickened
values.description: Status penalty equal to the value to all checks and DCs. Spend 1 action and Fortitude save to reduce.

values.label: Stunned
values.description: Lose actions equal to the value (or up to the Stunned value) at the start of your turn; reduce Stunned by the actions lost.

values.label: Slowed
values.description: Lose actions equal to the value at the start of each turn (does not stack with Stunned for the lost-action calculation; take the worse).

values.label: Quickened
values.description: Gain 1 extra action at the start of each turn, usable only for the specified purpose granted by the source.

values.label: Prone
values.description: -2 circumstance to attacks; off-guard. Standing up is a single action.

values.label: Grabbed
values.description: Off-guard and immobilized; you can Escape (Acrobatics or Athletics, or attempt a basic check) as an action.

values.label: Restrained
values.description: Off-guard and immobilized; cannot use manipulate actions other than Escape or to break free.

values.label: Wounded
values.description: When dying value is reset, your wounded value increases. Future dying tracks add wounded value to the new dying.

values.label: Dying
values.description: Drop to 0 HP and you become dying 1 (or higher if crit hit). At dying 4 you die. Each turn make a recovery flat check (DC 10 + dying).

values.label: Doomed
values.description: Maximum dying value drops by your doomed value. At doomed 4 (or whatever brings dying max to 0), if you would become dying you die.

values.label: Confused
values.description: You attack any creature in reach (or use cantrip on a random adjacent target if no melee). You're off-guard.

values.label: Fascinated
values.description: -2 status to Perception and skill checks; cannot Concentrate or use actions with concentrate trait other than Seek; ends if hostile action is taken against you.

values.label: Drained
values.description: Status penalty equal to value to Constitution-based checks, Fortitude saves, and HP max. Lose HP equal to drained × level.

values.label: Enfeebled
values.description: Status penalty equal to value to Strength-based rolls and damage.

values.label: Stupefied
values.description: Status penalty equal to value to Intelligence/Wisdom/Charisma checks and to spell DCs and spell attack rolls.

values.label: Clumsy
values.description: Status penalty equal to value to Dexterity-based rolls and to AC.

values.label: Persistent Damage
values.description: At end of each turn take damage of the listed type; flat check DC 15 (or DC 10 with assistance) to end.

values.label: Unconscious
values.description: Off-guard; -4 status to AC, Perception, and Reflex; cannot act. Wakes if HP restored or shaken (1 action) and DC 5 Perception.

lorebook.name: Pathfinder 2e Rules Reference
lorebook.description: Keyword-triggered reference for Pathfinder 2nd Edition (Remaster) classes, conditions, and core mechanics. Surface-level rules reference for the GM agent — fires when keywords appear in narration. Original mechanical references; Pathfinder 2e content under Paizo's ORC license.

entries.name: Mechanic: Four Degrees of Success
entries.content: Every check resolves to one of four degrees. Critical Success: rolled total >= DC + 10 (or natural 20 bumping a Success up). Success: total >= DC. Failure: total < DC. Critical Failure: total <= DC - 10 (or natural 1 bumping a Failure down). Natural 20 shifts degree up one step; natural 1 shifts down one step (apply AFTER computing the DC delta). Most pf2e abilities have distinct effects on all four lines — never collapse them. Save spells often use the 'basic save' pattern: crit save = no damage, save = half, fail = full, crit fail = double.

entries.name: Mechanic: Three-Action Economy
entries.content: Each turn you have 3 actions, 1 reaction, and any number of free actions. Actions cost 1, 2, or 3. Most attacks (Strike) are 1 action. Most spells are 2 actions. Mode-changing or extended activities take 3 actions. Reactions trigger on a cue (Attack of Opportunity, Shield Block) and reset at the start of your next turn. Free actions cost no action but each has a trigger or per-round limit. Common single actions: Strike, Stride, Step (1 square no trigger), Demoralize, Recall Knowledge, Trip, Shove, Grapple, Raise a Shield, Take Cover.

entries.name: Mechanic: Multiple Attack Penalty (MAP)
entries.content: Your 2nd attack action this turn takes -5 to its attack roll; your 3rd takes -10. Weapons with the agile trait reduce these to -4 and -8. MAP resets at the start of each turn and applies only to attack-trait actions (Strike, Trip, Shove, Grapple, Disarm), not to damage. The third attack at -10 almost never lands against an at-level foe — most turns you should spend that third action on Demoralize, Raise a Shield, Stride, or Recall Knowledge instead of a 3rd Strike.

entries.name: Mechanic: Proficiency Tiers
entries.content: Five proficiency tiers add to checks: Untrained = 0 (no -2 penalty in Remaster), Trained = level + 2, Expert = level + 4, Master = level + 6, Legendary = level + 8. Tiers gate access to special trained-only actions (e.g. Treat Wounds requires trained Medicine; Identify Magic requires trained tradition skill). At higher tiers you also get access to Master- and Legendary-only feats and spells.

entries.name: Mechanic: Saving Throws
entries.content: Three saves: Fortitude (CON-based, against poison/disease/physical resistance), Reflex (DEX-based, against area effects/dodge), Will (WIS-based, against mental/charm). Each is d20 + ability_mod + proficiency_bonus + item_bonus. Most damage spells use the 'basic save' pattern (crit save = no damage, save = half, fail = full, crit fail = double). Other save effects use unique outcome blocks per spell — read the spell's text.

entries.name: Mechanic: Hero Points
entries.content: Players start each session with 1 Hero Point (cap 3). Spend 1 Hero Point as a free action to either: reroll one check just made, taking the better result; OR remove the dying condition and stabilize at 0 HP. The GM hands out additional Hero Points for clever solutions, table-positive play, dramatic risks, accepting compels, or spending the spotlight on someone else. Hero Points reset at session start; never accumulate across sessions.

entries.name: Mechanic: Recall Knowledge
entries.content: A single action with the Concentrate trait. Roll the appropriate skill (Lore, Arcana, Crafting, Nature, Occultism, Religion, Society) vs. a level-based DC, usually the creature's or topic's level converted to the standard DC table. Critical Success: one accurate fact plus useful context. Success: one accurate fact (GM picks the most useful for the situation). Failure: no useful info. Critical Failure: GM tells a believable lie that the character thinks is true. Players ask 'what do I know about X' — they don't pre-specify what they want to learn.

entries.name: Conditions: Off-Guard, Frightened, Sickened, Stunned, Slowed
entries.content: Off-Guard: -2 circumstance to AC. Triggered by being prone, flanked, hidden from, grabbed, or many maneuvers. (Replaces 5e/old-pf2e 'flat-footed'.) Frightened N: -N status penalty to all checks and DCs; reduces by 1 at end of each turn. Sickened N: -N status to all checks and DCs; spend an action and Fort save to reduce. Stunned N: lose actions equal to N (or up to N) at start of turn; reduce Stunned by actions lost. Slowed N: lose N actions at start of turn (does not stack with Stunned for the lost-action calc — take the worse).

entries.name: Conditions: Wounded, Dying, Doomed
entries.content: Drop to 0 HP and you become Dying 1 (Dying 2 if from a critical hit). On your turn while Dying you make a recovery flat check vs. DC 10 + dying value: crit success reduces dying by 2, success by 1, failure increases by 1, crit failure by 2. At Dying 4 you die. When the dying condition is removed, gain Wounded 1 (or +1 to existing Wounded). Future Dying conditions add your Wounded value to the new Dying value. Doomed N reduces your maximum Dying value by N — at Doomed 4 (or whatever brings max Dying to 0) you die when you would gain dying. Hero Point can spend to remove Dying and stabilize at 0 HP.

entries.name: Conditions: Drained, Enfeebled, Stupefied, Clumsy
entries.content: Pf2e doesn't use ability score damage; it uses status conditions. Drained N: -N to CON-based checks and Fort saves; lose N × level HP (max HP also reduced by that amount). Enfeebled N: -N to STR-based rolls and damage. Stupefied N: -N to INT/WIS/CHA checks and to spell DCs and spell attack rolls. Clumsy N: -N to DEX-based rolls and to AC. All four reduce by 1 per day naturally (or on a successful save where applicable).

entries.name: Combat: Initiative & Combat Round
entries.content: Initiative is a single skill check at the start of an encounter — usually Perception, but the GM may call for Stealth (if you're sneaking) or another skill (Deception when feinting at a parley). Higher result acts first. NPCs are usually grouped — the GM rolls one initiative for a squad of similar enemies. Turn structure: each character takes 3 actions in their turn slot, can take their reaction once between turns. The Delay action lets you change your initiative order downward.

entries.name: Combat: Attack Rolls & Damage
entries.content: Strike action: 1 action, roll d20 + ability mod (STR for non-finesse melee, DEX for finesse or ranged) + weapon proficiency + item bonus vs. target's AC. Apply MAP for second/third Strikes. Critical hit (Crit Success on attack) doubles damage and applies the weapon's critical specialization effect. Damage: weapon's listed dice + ability mod (STR for melee; STR for thrown weapons; no ability mod for non-propulsive ranged like crossbows; HALF STR mod for propulsive ranged like composite bows). Many weapons have traits (deadly, fatal, forceful, sweep, etc.) that modify the math.

entries.name: Combat: Spellcasting Basics
entries.content: Spells have a rank 1-10. Cantrips auto-heighten to half caster level rounded up. Most spells are 2 actions (verbal + somatic + targeting). Spell DC = 10 + spellcasting proficiency bonus + casting ability mod. Spell attack rolls = d20 + spellcasting prof + casting ability. Casting traditions: Arcane (wizards, magus), Divine (clerics, champions), Occult (bards, psychics), Primal (druids, rangers). Slots are per rank per day; lower-rank slots can heighten lower-rank spells. Sustained spells require a 1-action Sustain each subsequent turn.

entries.name: Classes: Fighter / Rogue / Wizard / Cleric / Druid / Bard / Ranger / Champion / Barbarian / Sorcerer
entries.content: Fighter: 10 HP/level, weapon legend. Best martial proficiency progression in the game. Reactive Strike at level 1. Rogue: 8 HP/level, skill specialist. Sneak Attack 1d6 (scales). High skill bumps. Wizard: 6 HP/level, Int caster. Prepared arcane spells from a spellbook. Curriculum focus. Cleric: 8 HP/level, Wis caster. Prepared divine spells. Channels: Heal/Harm Font. Druid: 8 HP/level, Wis caster. Prepared primal spells. Order specialization (Animal/Leaf/Storm/etc.). Bard: 8 HP/level, Cha caster. Spontaneous occult; Composition cantrip Inspire Courage. Ranger: 10 HP/level, Hunt Prey + class-specific edge (Flurry/Outwit/Precision). Pure martial by default; specific subclass features and feats can grant primal spells but Ranger is NOT a default spellcaster. Champion: 10 HP/level, divine cause + reactive ability tied to your cause's edicts, anathema, and tenets (Remaster removed alignment; causes are now Justice/Liberation/Obedience/Redemption/Grandeur/Iniquity defined by their tenets). Barbarian: 12 HP/level, Rage; instinct (Animal/Dragon/Spirit/etc.). Sorcerer: 6 HP/level, bloodline-driven spontaneous caster, tradition matches bloodline.

entries.name: Ancestries: Human / Elf / Dwarf / Halfling / Goblin / Gnome / Orc / Leshy / Tengu / Kobold
entries.content: Pf2e replaces 'race' with 'ancestry'. Each grants HP at level 1, base speed, languages, vision, and a heritage choice plus 1 ancestry feat. Human: 8 ancestry HP, 25 ft, Common + 1, versatile heritage options. Elf: 6, 30 ft, low-light/darkvision via heritage, long-lived. Dwarf: 10, 20 ft, darkvision, sturdy. Halfling: 6, 25 ft, keen eyes. Goblin: 6, 25 ft, darkvision, junkyard scavenger flavor. Gnome: 8, 25 ft, low-light, fey-touched. Orc: 10, 25 ft, darkvision, ferocity. Leshy: 8, 25 ft, plant-folk. Tengu: 6, 25 ft, low-light, bird-folk martial heritage. Kobold: 6, 25 ft, draconic heritage. Each has 1d8 (or class-determined) HP per level on top.

entries.name: Mechanic: Bonus & Penalty Stacking
entries.content: Pf2e has four bonus/penalty types: item, circumstance, status, and untyped. SAME-TYPE BONUSES DO NOT STACK — only the highest of each type applies. Same-type penalties don't stack either — only the worst applies. Untyped bonuses and penalties always stack. This is what keeps the math tight: even a +1 status from Bless plus a +1 status from Aid does NOT become +2 — only +1 applies. Always tag bonuses by type so the player and GM know what stacks.

entries.name: Healing & Recovery
entries.content: Treat Wounds: trained Medicine, 10 minutes per target, DC depends on Medicine proficiency target. Heals 2d8 + extras (more on crit, less on fail; can't be retried on the same target for 1 hour after success, 10 min after failure). 8-hour rest restores HP equal to constitution_mod × level (min 1 × level), removes fatigued, reduces drained/wounded by 1. Refocus is a 10-minute activity that recovers 1 Focus Point (cap is class-dependent). Hero Points reset to 1 at session start, NOT at long rest.
